queryExamples: How much are we spending monthly?
Which vendors put us over budget?
What can we cut to get under budget?
notes: Confirmed from Gmail receipts and the subscription audit tracker; unresolved usage-based tools still require dashboard verification.
Recent quick enrollments/cancellations may make next-month realized spend lower than this snapshot.
2026-05-10 provider refresh kept the budget status yellow: no cancellation approval was requested and no vendor crossed the cancellation threshold.
2026-05-11 weekly refresh kept the budget status yellow: n8n, Supabase, and Vercel showed fresh operational movement; no vendor crossed the cancellation threshold.
2026-05-12 daily refresh kept the budget status yellow: n8n and Supabase moved again, Vercel project metadata did not refresh from CLI output, and no vendor crossed the cancellation threshold.

2026-05-13 daily refresh kept the budget status yellow: n8n, Supabase, Vercel, Stripe, and HeyGen had live read-only signals; OpenRouter stayed zero-usage and no vendor crossed the cancellation threshold.
Cancellation remains approval-gated; this budget view is read-only.

2026-05-18 weekly refresh kept the budget status yellow: n8n, Supabase local-project samples, Vercel, Stripe, Slack, OpenAI, Anthropic, Apify, HeyGen, Pinecone, Printful, Hunter, and ElevenLabs had readable signals; OpenRouter stayed zero-usage, Calendly auth remained blocked, Gemini returned 403, Read AI/Resend/Vapi local keys were absent, and no vendor crossed the cancellation threshold.

2026-05-18 daily refresh kept the budget status yellow: Supabase, n8n, Vercel, Stripe, Slack, OpenAI, Anthropic, Apify, HeyGen, Pinecone, Printful, Hunter, and ElevenLabs had live read-only signals; OpenRouter stayed zero-usage, Vapi returned no current sampled calls, Calendly auth remained blocked, Gemini returned 403, Read AI lacked a local API key, and no vendor crossed the cancellation threshold.

2026-05-19 daily refresh kept the budget status yellow: Supabase local-project samples, n8n, Vercel, Stripe, Slack, OpenAI, Anthropic, Apify, HeyGen, Pinecone, Printful, Hunter, ElevenLabs, and Read AI had live read-only signals; OpenRouter stayed zero-usage, Vapi returned no sampled calls, Calendly auth remained blocked, Gemini returned 403, Resend local key was absent, and no vendor crossed the cancellation threshold.

2026-05-23 daily refresh kept the budget status yellow: Supabase, n8n, Vercel, Stripe, Slack, OpenAI, Anthropic, Apify, HeyGen, Pinecone, Printful, Hunter, ElevenLabs, and Read AI had live read-only signals; OpenRouter stayed zero-usage, Vapi returned no sampled calls, Resend local key was absent, Calendly auth remained blocked, Gemini returned 403, and no vendor crossed the approval-ready cancellation threshold.

2026-05-24 daily refresh kept the budget status yellow: Supabase analytics moved, n8n had five successful executions around 2026-05-24T12:00Z, Vercel, Stripe, Slack, OpenAI, Anthropic, Apify, HeyGen, Pinecone, Printful, Hunter, ElevenLabs, and Read AI had live or readable signals; OpenRouter stayed zero-usage, Vapi returned no sampled calls, Resend local key was absent, Calendly auth remained blocked, Gemini returned 403, and no vendor crossed the approval-ready cancellation threshold.

2026-05-25 daily refresh kept the budget status yellow: local Supabase analytics and agent_runs moved, n8n had five successful executions around 2026-05-25T12:00Z, Vercel, Stripe, Slack, OpenAI, Anthropic, Apify, HeyGen, Pinecone, Printful, Hunter, ElevenLabs, and Read AI had live or readable signals; production Supabase was quiet since the prior sample; OpenRouter stayed zero-usage, Vapi returned no sampled calls, Resend local key was absent, Calendly auth remained blocked, Gemini returned 403, and no vendor crossed the approval-ready cancellation threshold.

2026-05-25 weekly refresh kept the budget status yellow: Read AI improved from the prior weekly auth-unresolved state to connector-readable May meeting evidence, Vapi improved from missing-key to API-readable but quiet, ElevenLabs entered a new zero-usage reset cycle, BuiltWith stayed a protected outreach-ramp watch item, Fireflies stayed resolved/canceled, and no vendor crossed the approval-ready cancellation threshold.

transitionAdjustments.rationale: Claude Max appears in the current receipt-backed run-rate, but the ChatGPT switch should remove this line from the next settled cycle if cancellation remains in place.

apifyCallAnalysis.lastMonitorExecution: Direct provider refresh on 2026-05-25 was readable; sampled account-level Apify runs were mixed and dated 2026-04-29, 2026-05-06, and 2026-05-13, while prior 2026-05-20 account-level activity and actor-level bakeoff evidence remain the active replacement-analysis base.
apifyCallAnalysis.nextAction: Keep productive evidence actors under watch, pause or replace no-run/failing/empty-output actors, and compare the remaining productive categories against cheaper alternatives before renewal.

actorRunHistory.label: Facebook friends
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Pause unless a campaign-owned Facebook source is explicitly active; browser/manual review is safer before re-enabling.

actorRunHistory.label: Facebook groups
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Pause by default and replace with manual group review or approved community exports if needed.

actorRunHistory.label: Facebook comments
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Pause unless a specific post-engagement campaign justifies a controlled run.

actorRunHistory.label: LinkedIn connections
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Replace or keep disabled; repeated failures with zero output make this a poor use of Apify.

actorRunHistory.label: LinkedIn post engagement
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Investigate input quality before keeping; success with zero items is not useful output.

actorRunHistory.label: Reddit listening
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Keep for now; run a bakeoff against Reddit API/RSS/search before replacing.

actorRunHistory.label: Google Maps
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Keep only if Google Places API or manual CSV cannot produce equivalent accepted evidence.

actorRunHistory.label: LinkedIn post search
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Keep as the strongest current Apify value signal; compare against browser-agent sampling.

actorRunHistory.label: G2 reviews
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Replace with manual/source-registered review capture unless the actor can return non-empty datasets.

actorRunHistory.label: Capterra reviews
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Keep only for targeted evidence collection; run a manual/browser-search replacement test.

actorRunHistory.label: Profile enrichment
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Remove from active watch if the older HeyGen cold-email flow is retired.

actorRunHistory.label: Website screenshot/video
actorRunHistory.recommendation: Replace with local Playwright/Browser Use/Vercel Chromium capture if website capture is still needed.

summary.headline: Weekly refresh: Supabase local samples and n8n stayed active, Read AI is connector-readable again, Vapi is API-readable but quiet, ElevenLabs reset to a zero-usage cycle, BuiltWith remains protected watch, Fireflies remains canceled, and no vendor reached the approval-ready cancellation threshold.
nextReviewFocus: Review ElevenLabs current-cycle zero usage against the next planned campaign before the 2026-06-19 reset.
Confirm OpenRouter role in the next media/model bakeoff before preparing any deprecation packet.
Verify Vapi dashboard billing and whether production voice UX should stay enabled.
Check Printful dashboard order history and decide whether both native stores remain active.
Verify whether production uses Resend or Gmail/n8n as the real outbound channel.
Check Pinecone billing/API traffic against Supabase/local RAG usage.
Refresh Calendly token and rerun event-type usage checks.
Resolve Gemini/Google AI permission status before relying on image/social workflows.
Continue the Apify actor-level replacement bakeoff by pausing weak surfaces before account-level changes.
Keep BuiltWith active during the outreach ramp and judge it only against lead-prep and conversion evidence.

vendors.name: Vapi
vendors.billingSignal: Dashboard billing still needs verification; VAPI_PRIVATE_KEY was present and the 2026-05-25 default call sample returned zero calls again; prior visible call remains 2026-04-30.
vendors.usageSignal: No meaningful current Vapi usage appeared again on 2026-05-25; 189 local references remain, including optional website voice paths.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Voice/audit experience risk if production voice UX is enabled.
vendors.recommendation: Investigate billing and intended voice UX; prepare a deprecation packet only if dashboard spend exists and voice UX is not needed.
vendors.nextAction: Check Vapi dashboard billing and decide whether the voice path should stay enabled.

vendors.name: BuiltWith
vendors.billingSignal: Standing decision: keep active during the outreach/client-volume ramp despite being the largest tracked budget lever.
vendors.usageSignal: Usefulness should be judged against lead prep, audits, implementation strategies, proposals, client outcomes, and conversion evidence, not isolated repo activity.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Outreach and client-preparation enrichment may depend on stack data during ramp.
vendors.recommendation: Keep active during outreach ramp; reassess only after enough sales evidence exists.
vendors.nextAction: Compare BuiltWith-enriched work against actual sales prep and conversion outcomes.

vendors.name: Fireflies.ai
vendors.billingSignal: Already canceled per Vambah confirmation on 2026-05-01; no new paid-plan evidence appeared in this run.
vendors.usageSignal: Only one local reference remains; keep out of the active queue unless paid-plan evidence reappears.
vendors.recommendation: Keep out of active cancellation queue unless new paid-plan evidence appears.
vendors.nextAction: No action unless a new receipt, dashboard signal, or code dependency appears.

vendors.name: Vercel
vendors.billingSignal: Vercel CLI was authenticated as vsillah and listed current portfolio and portfolio-staging deployment URLs; the Vercel connector still returned a scope 403.
vendors.usageSignal: Active production and staging hosting/deployment dependency with 731 local references excluding tracker files, lockfile, tmp, and media noise.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Core hosting, previews, production, and staging verification.
vendors.recommendation: Keep; use CLI evidence when connector auth is blocked or unavailable.
vendors.nextAction: Continue verifying both portfolio and portfolio-staging deployment contexts during merge/release work.

vendors.name: Printful
vendors.billingSignal: Printful API returned two native stores, but both sampled Supabase printful_sync_log tables remain empty.
vendors.usageSignal: Merch/store code and webhook references remain; no sync movement was observed in the 2026-05-25 database samples.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Merchandise/order fulfillment if the store strategy is active.
vendors.recommendation: Investigate dashboard order history and merchandise strategy before any deprecation decision.
vendors.nextAction: Check Printful dashboard orders and decide whether both native stores remain strategically active.

vendors.name: Resend
vendors.billingSignal: RESEND_API_KEY was absent locally on 2026-05-25; production env and billing were not verified.
vendors.usageSignal: Optional code/env/webhook references remain, but Gmail/n8n remains the visible outbound path in repo references.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Transactional email deliverability if Resend is enabled in production.
vendors.recommendation: Verify production env and billing before keeping as a paid dependency or removing code paths.
vendors.nextAction: Check Vercel production env/billing for Resend and confirm whether Gmail/n8n is the real outbound channel.

vendors.name: Pinecone
vendors.billingSignal: Pinecone API returned one ready serverless index named publications in aws/us-east-1; billing/API traffic was not available from this API sample.
vendors.usageSignal: RAG ingestion exports and 323 local references remain; documents_local_rag also exists in Supabase/local samples.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Publication/RAG search path if Pinecone remains the live vector store.
vendors.recommendation: Investigate billing/API traffic and compare with Supabase/local RAG replacement path.
vendors.nextAction: Check Pinecone usage/billing dashboard before any replacement or cancellation packet.

vendors.name: Apify
vendors.billingSignal: Account-level API remained readable; current five-run sample was mixed and not newer than 2026-05-13, while prior $39 monthly receipt evidence remains the tracked billing signal.
vendors.usageSignal: n8n exports still include Apify surfaces; actor-level evidence remains mixed with productive, failed, and no-run surfaces.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Lead enrichment, social listening, review capture, and actor-health workflows where alternatives are not yet proven.
vendors.recommendation: Keep account-level subscription for now; pause or replace weak actor surfaces before any account-level decision.
vendors.nextAction: Continue actor-level replacement bakeoff and disable weak surfaces before considering account cancellation.

vendors.name: Hunter.io
vendors.billingSignal: Hunter API reports Free plan with 75 available calls and 50 used calls.
vendors.usageSignal: Low reference count and no paid-plan evidence in this run.
vendors.recommendation: Keep/watch; not a paid cancellation target unless the account upgrades or becomes redundant.
vendors.nextAction: Recheck only if Hunter usage increases or paid-plan evidence appears.

vendors.name: OpenRouter
vendors.billingSignal: Current key returned status 200 with usage 0 again on 2026-05-25; paid spend was not confirmed.
vendors.usageSignal: Consecutive quiet key evidence; 106 local references remain excluding tracker files, lockfile, tmp, and media noise.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Potential model-router or bakeoff fallback; no current usage signal from the sampled key.
vendors.recommendation: Watch until bakeoff evidence confirms whether it is active, sandbox-only, or replaceable; prepare a deprecation packet before cancellation.
vendors.nextAction: Confirm OpenRouter role in the next media/model bakeoff and verify active spend.

vendors.name: ElevenLabs
vendors.billingSignal: ElevenLabs API reports active Creator subscription with 0 of 246,034 characters used in the current cycle and next reset on 2026-06-19.
vendors.usageSignal: Current cycle remains quiet, but social/audio campaign dependencies may still exist.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Voiceover/audio generation for social content and media campaigns.
vendors.recommendation: Watch through the next campaign decision; do not cancel without confirming no near-term audio/video plan.
vendors.nextAction: Review current-cycle zero usage against the next planned social/audio/video campaign before 2026-06-19.

vendors.name: Read.ai
vendors.billingSignal: Prior receipt-backed Read Pro evidence remains; billing dashboard was not queried in this run.
vendors.usageSignal: Read AI connector returned three May meetings again, with latest sampled meeting on 2026-05-15.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Meeting transcript, recap, and client-context workflows.
vendors.recommendation: Keep while meeting transcript workflow is active; use dashboard billing verification only for cost-cut decisions.
vendors.nextAction: Continue using connector evidence as usage signal; verify billing only if cost-cut decisions are needed.

vendors.name: Calendly
vendors.billingSignal: Prior Standard receipt remains the billing signal; direct API check returned 401 again on 2026-05-25.
vendors.usageSignal: Scheduling references and n8n Calendly trigger exports remain.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Discovery, onboarding, kickoff, progress, renewal, and delivery scheduling links.
vendors.recommendation: Keep as low-cost scheduling infrastructure unless a replacement path is approved.
vendors.nextAction: Refresh Calendly token and rerun event-type usage checks.

vendors.name: Gemini / Google AI
vendors.billingSignal: Gemini model-list check returned 403 again on 2026-05-25.
vendors.usageSignal: Social image workflow references remain, but current key/project status is not usable from this sample.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Gemini/image generation and RAG chatbot experiments if still active.
vendors.recommendation: Investigate key/project status before treating Gemini as a reliable active provider or cancellation target.
vendors.nextAction: Resolve Google AI key/project permissions before depending on Gemini workflows.

vendors.name: Anthropic
vendors.billingSignal: Anthropic model list remained readable with 9 sampled models; receipt-backed Claude Max transition watch remains unresolved.
vendors.usageSignal: Code and n8n fallback/eval references remain, but budget decision depends on next settled receipt and model bakeoff refresh.
vendors.portfolioDependency: LLM fallback/evaluation paths and any Claude Max workflow still in use.
vendors.recommendation: Watch through the next receipt and model bakeoff refresh before deprecation.
vendors.nextAction: Confirm whether the ChatGPT switch removed the Anthropic subscription line from the next settled cycle.

vendors.name: Figma / Paper / Excalidraw / Canva
vendors.billingSignal: No billing evidence was gathered in this repo/API run; local references were low: Figma 2, Paper 24, Excalidraw 4, Canva 4.
vendors.usageSignal: Design/media references exist but do not prove paid Portfolio subscriptions.
vendors.portfolioDependency: Design artifacts, diagrams, and visual content workflows if active outside repo code.
vendors.recommendation: Investigate only if receipts, dashboard evidence, or active design-production needs appear.
vendors.nextAction: Check receipts or tool dashboards only when a design/media subscription appears in spend evidence.
